transcript.text: What does this project do, and who relies on it?

Signal Relay is our telemetry platform. backend/ is the ingestion service customers' agents send telemetry to. client-lib/ is the Python SDK our other internal services use to call it — not distributed to external customers.
What's the deployment model and runtime environment for backend and for client-lib?
Both are plain Python — backend/ runs as a FastAPI service behind our internal load balancer, client-lib/ is installed as a library into other internal Python services. Neither is browser-facing.
I see backend/docs/compliance/SOC2-CONTROLS.md with a control mapping, and the root SECURITY.md gestures at a scoping split — is client-lib in the same SOC2 scope, or is that split intentional?

Backend-only. backend/ holds customer telemetry and the audit-log evidence trail, so it's SOC2-scoped. client-lib/ is just an HTTP wrapper — it doesn't store or process customer data itself, so it isn't in SOC2 scope. Don't apply the SBOM/audit-log requirements to client-lib/.
What's the audience, and does backend or client-lib handle sensitive data like customer telemetry?

Internal only — both subprojects are used by our own engineering org, not external customers. backend/ handles customer telemetry data in transit and at rest, which is why it's SOC2-scoped. client-lib/ just forwards requests; it doesn't persist anything.
Is this project AI-powered, or built with, or used by, LLM coding agents — for backend and for client-lib specifically?
Built day to day with Claude Code, but neither subproject calls an LLM at runtime or is itself invoked by one — plain telemetry ingestion and a plain HTTP client.
What minimum threshold should Karen enforce for backend and for client-lib?
80% for both subprojects, same as our other internal services.
Got it. backend gets the SOC2 artifact and audit-log checks scoped to it alone; client-lib stays out of that scope. Generating the harness now.

subprojects.reason: Ingests customer telemetry and holds the tamper-evident audit-log/SBOM evidence trail described in backend/docs/compliance/SOC2-CONTROLS.md — SOC2-scoped on its own, independent of the repo root profile

Thin HTTP client SDK forwarded to backend/ over HTTPS — holds no customer data itself and carries none of backend's SOC2 artifact requirements; explicitly not compliance-scoped despite sharing a repo with a subproject that is

gateScripts.gate-1-supply-chain:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
for mod in backend client-lib; do
  report="$(cd "$root/$mod" && pip-audit --format json 2>/dev/null || true)"
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$mod/$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(echo "$report" | python3 "$root/.karen/gates/format_pip_audit.py")
done
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-2-completeness:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
for mod in backend client-lib; do
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(cd "$root/$mod" && ruff check src --select F,E --output-format=concise 2>/dev/null | python3 "$root/.karen/gates/format_ruff.py")
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(python3 "$root/.karen/gates/stub_scan.py" "$root/$mod/src")
done
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-3-security:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
for mod in backend client-lib; do
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(cd "$root/$mod" && bandit -r src -f json 2>/dev/null | python3 "$root/.karen/gates/format_bandit.py")
done
# working-tree secret scan, root-wide, not scoped to tracked files only
while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
done < <(gitleaks detect --source "$root" --no-git --report-format json 2>/dev/null | python3 "$root/.karen/gates/format_gitleaks.py")
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; echo "ZERO-TOLERANCE"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-4-docs-parity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
for mod in backend client-lib; do
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(node "$root/.karen/gates/doctest-and-symbol-diff.js" "$root/$mod")
done
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-5-compliance:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
# SOC2 artifacts checked against backend/ only -- client-lib is explicitly
# out of SOC2 scope per the interview.
required=("backend/docs/compliance/SOC2-CONTROLS.md")
for f in "${required[@]}"; do
  if [ ! -f "$root/$f" ]; then
    out+="$f:0"$'\t'"required SOC2 artifact missing"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  fi
done
if ! grep -q "Generate SBOM" "$root/.github/workflows/ci.yml" 2>/dev/null; then
  out+=".github/workflows/ci.yml:0"$'\t'"no SBOM generation step found for backend release artifact"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
fi
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-6-test-integrity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
for mod in backend client-lib; do
  pkg=$([ "$mod" = "backend" ] && echo signal_relay_backend || echo signal_relay_client)
  (cd "$root/$mod" && pytest --cov="$pkg" --cov-report=xml >/dev/null 2>&1) || true
  pct=$(python3 "$root/.karen/gates/coverage_pct.py" "$root/$mod/coverage.xml")
  threshold=80
  if [ -n "$pct" ] && awk -v p="$pct" -v t="$threshold" 'BEGIN{exit !(p<t)}'; then
    out+="$mod/coverage.xml:0"$'\t'"coverage $pct% below $threshold% threshold"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  fi
done
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi
gateScripts.gate-7-agent-context:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root="$1"
issues=0
out=""
agents_file="$root/AGENTS.md"
if [ ! -f "$agents_file" ]; then
  out+="AGENTS.md:0"$'\t'"missing root agent-context file"$'\n'
  issues=$((issues+1))
else
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
    [ -z "$file" ] && continue
    out+="$file:$line"$'\t'"$msg"$'\n'
    issues=$((issues+1))
  done < <(python3 "$root/.karen/gates/agent_context_checklist.py" "$agents_file" backend client-lib)
fi
printf '%s' "$out"
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then echo "PASS (0 issues)"; exit 0; else echo "FAIL ($issues issues)"; exit 1; fi

backend/src/signal_relay_backend/cache.py:22	pickle.loads on cached Redis blob sharing a keyspace with operator-triggered diagnostic output — unsafe deserialization of untrusted data
backend/src/signal_relay_backend/server.py:30	subprocess.run with shell=True and unsanitized operator-supplied probe string — shell injection via /admin/diagnostics
client-lib/src/signal_relay_client/config.py:8	hardcoded fallback API key checked into source, used as ClientConfig default — credential leak via repository
client-lib/src/signal_relay_client/filters.py:17	eval() on caller-supplied filter expression with full builtins available — dynamic code execution
client-lib/src/signal_relay_client/filters.py:31	literal string "eval(" appears in documentation example text -- flagged by textual-only heuristic that does not recognize string-literal context
